heading: PROCLAMATION OF STATE OF EMERGENCY
operative_text: On application of the chief executive officer or governing body of a county or municipality during an emergency, the governor may proclaim a state of emergency and designate the area involved. For the purposes of this section an emergency exists in the following situations: (1) a riot or unlawful assembly by three or more persons acting together by use of force or violence; (2) if a clear and present danger of the use of violence exists; or (3) a natural or man-made disaster.

defined_terms.definition_excerpt:
For the purposes of this section an emergency exists in the following situations:
(1) a riot or unlawful assembly by three or more persons acting together by use of force or violence;
(2) if a clear and present danger of the use of violence exists; or
(3) a natural or man-made disaster.

_note: Acts 1987, 70th Leg., ch. 147, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1987. omitted from operative_text as enactment history.
